source_constraint: Findings must map to a rule ID (SM-*) defined in this skill's reference files. Sui semantics verifiable at https://docs.sui.io and https://move-book.com.

evals.expected_output: Flags that AdminCap has the `copy` ability, making the capability duplicable.
expectations: Identifies the issue as SM-A1 (capability ability hygiene)
Explains that `copy` lets an attacker duplicate the AdminCap and replay privileged calls
Rates it Critical

evals.expected_output: Flags a privileged, state-mutating entry function with no capability or sender authorization.
expectations: Identifies SM-A2 (missing authorization)
Notes any address can call withdraw_all and drain the shared vault
Rates it Critical

evals.expected_output: Flags that PoolCap is not bound to a specific Pool, so one cap authorizes actions on any pool.
expectations: Identifies SM-A3 (capability not bound to the resource it governs)
Explains the confused-deputy / cross-pool exploit: own one cap, pass someone else's pool
Suggests storing the governed pool's ID in the cap and asserting it

evals.expected_output: Flags that `store` makes the 'soulbound' badge freely transferable via public_transfer.
expectations: Identifies SM-B2 (irreversible store breaks soulbound)
Explains that store enables public_transfer/public_share from any module
Recommends removing `store`

evals.expected_output: Flags that `p: &PriceObject` is not verified to be the canonical price object; an attacker can pass a self-created PriceObject.
expectations: Identifies SM-B4 (type confusion / fake-object injection)
Explains attacker mints their own PriceObject with an inflated value to over-borrow
Recommends checking object::id against a trusted/registered oracle

evals.expected_output: Flags that the TreasuryCap is shared, so anyone can take &mut and mint unlimited coins.
expectations: Identifies SM-G1 (mint/burn & treasury custody)
Explains that sharing the TreasuryCap lets any caller mint arbitrarily
Rates it Critical

evals.expected_output: Flags that State has a version field but entrypoints never assert it, so old package versions keep mutating new state after an upgrade.
expectations: Identifies SM-I2 (versioning / migration gap)
Explains that without assert!(s.version == CURRENT) an outdated package version can still mutate state
Notes init does not re-run on upgrade

evals.expected_output: Flags that draw is a public function returning a value derived from randomness, enabling test-and-abort.
expectations: Identifies SM-L2 (randomness test-and-abort)
Explains the attacker wraps draw in a PTB and aborts the transaction when roll >= 5, retrying until they win
Recommends making it a private entry function that finalizes the outcome without returning/revealing it
Rates it Critical

evals.expected_output: Flags that mint_for_testing is public but not gated by #[test_only], so it ships in published bytecode.
expectations: Identifies SM-M1 (test helper leakage)
Explains it becomes an unauthenticated unlimited-mint function on-chain
Recommends adding #[test_only]

evals.expected_output: Reports no critical/high vulnerability; the cap has correct abilities and the mutator is gated and bounds-checked.
expectations: Does NOT invent a critical finding
May note SM-A3 as a defense-in-depth consideration only if the cap should be bound to a specific Registry, but does not over-flag
Recognizes AdminCap abilities (no copy/drop) are correct
